testReportSummary: The RetryPolicy class in Configuration/ is fully implemented with exponential backoff and jitter, configurable MaxRetries/BaseDelay/MaxDelay, and ExecuteAsync wrappers. Build succeeded on first restore. All 117 coder unit tests pass, covering defaults, GetDelay growth/capping/jitter, ExecuteAsync retry logic, void overload, shouldRetry filtering, cancellation, and config validation. 11 new integration tests were added verifying file-based I/O retry, zero-retry policy (exactly one attempt), observable wall-clock delay, concurrent independent policies, cancellation token propagation into operations, and per-attempt delay bounds. The application starts and prints usage correctly. No bugs or regressions found.
reviewIssues: [MAJOR] `GetDelay` caps exponent with `Math.Min(attempt, 30)`, so attempts >30 stop growing even when `MaxDelay` is large. This violates exponential backoff semantics for higher retry counts.

[MAJOR] No validation for `BaseDelay`, `MaxDelay`, or `MaxRetries`. Negative `BaseDelay`/`MaxDelay` can produce negative delays and cause `Task.Delay` to throw unexpectedly; add guards and tests for invalid configuration values.
